articles.title: Stock futures fall after Trump says Iran war will continue for weeks: Live updates - CNBC
articles.description: All three major averages rose on Wednesday to kick off the second quarter.

articles.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX files to go public, seeking massive market debut - washingtonpost.com
articles.description: The aerospace company, valued at more than $1 trillion, could break the record for the largest public offering in history, according to reports.

articles.description: Amazon has dozens of items to fill your Easter basket, from toys to tech to everyday essentials.

articles.title: Shoe brand once worth $4B closes all stores, avoids bankruptcy - thestreet.com
articles.description: After issuing a going concern warning, the brand closed all stores but found a lifeline to avoid bankruptcy.

articles.title: Roadway outdoor dining is back in NYC for 2026, but it's only a slice of what it once was - Gothamist
articles.description: The program is far smaller than during the pandemic. City leaders say they want to fix that.

articles.title: Food pantries serving nearly 40 Minnesota communities suddenly close - Star Tribune
articles.description: Ruby’s Pantry operated food shelves for more than two decades in dozens of Minnesota communities and in other Upper Midwestern states. The nonprofit said the operation was “no longer financially sustainable.”

articles.title: Intel to Pay $14 Billion to Buy Back Apollo Stake in Ireland Plant - Bloomberg.com
articles.description: Intel Corp., the chipmaker aiming to restore its manufacturing prowess, agreed to pay $14.2 billion to buy back half of a plant in Ireland that it had previously sold to Apollo Global Management.

articles.title: Which Brands Will Be Hardest Hit by FCC's Foreign Router Ban? Here's the List - PCMag

articles.title: FDA Approves Eli Lilly’s GLP-1 Pill - WIRED
articles.description: Eli Lilly’s once-daily Foundayo is the second obesity pill to receive FDA approval. It will compete with Novo Nordisk’s pill version of Wegovy.
